input.content:
name: Artifact Cleanup Script

on:
  push:
    branches: ["**"]
  pull_request:
    branches: ["**"]
  schedule:
    # Run weekly on Monday at 02:00 UTC (simulates scheduled cleanup)
    - cron: "0 2 * * 1"
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      dry_run:
        description: "Run in dry-run mode (no actual deletions)"
        required: false
        default: "true"

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Job 1: Run Pester unit tests for the cleanup script
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  test:
    name: Run Pester Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install PowerShell
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update -q
          sudo apt-get install -y powershell

      - name: Install Pester
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted
          Install-Module -Name Pester -MinimumVersion 5.0.0 -Force -Scope CurrentUser

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $config = New-PesterConfiguration
          $config.Run.Path = './ArtifactCleanup.Tests.ps1'
          $config.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          $config.TestResult.Enabled = $true
          $config.TestResult.OutputPath = 'test-results.xml'
          $config.TestResult.OutputFormat = 'NUnitXml'
          $result = Invoke-Pester -Configuration $config
          if ($result.FailedCount -gt 0) {
            Write-Error "Pester tests failed: $($result.FailedCount) failure(s)"
            exit 1
          }
          Write-Host "All $($result.PassedCount) tests passed."

      - name: Upload test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: pester-test-results
          path: test-results.xml

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Job 2: Demonstrate the cleanup script with mock artifact data
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  demonstrate:
    name: Demonstrate Artifact Cleanup
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install PowerShell
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update -q
          sudo apt-get install -y powershell

      - name: Run artifact cleanup (dry-run)
        shell: pwsh
        env:
          DRY_RUN: ${{ github.event.inputs.dry_run || 'true' }}
        run: |
          # Source the cleanup functions
          . ./ArtifactCleanup.ps1

          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          # Mock artifact data representing a typical CI artifact store
          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          $referenceDate = [datetime]"2026-04-09"

          $artifacts = @(
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-001"    -SizeBytes 52428800  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2025-12-01") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-002"    -SizeBytes 55574528  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-01-15") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-003"    -SizeBytes 57671680  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-02-20") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-004"    -SizeBytes 60817408  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-03-10") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-005"    -SizeBytes 62914560  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-04-01") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "test-report-001"   -SizeBytes 10485760  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-01-20") -WorkflowRunId "ci-test"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "test-report-002"   -SizeBytes 11534336  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-03-15") -WorkflowRunId "ci-test"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "test-report-003"   -SizeBytes 12582912  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-04-05") -WorkflowRunId "ci-test"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "deploy-staging-01" -SizeBytes 209715200 -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2025-11-01") -WorkflowRunId "cd-deploy"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "deploy-prod-01"    -SizeBytes 209715200 -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-04-08") -WorkflowRunId "cd-deploy"
          )

          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          # Retention policy
          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          $policy = @{
            MaxAgeDays        = 90        # delete anything older than 90 days

name: Artifact Cleanup Script

on:
  push:
    branches: ["**"]
  pull_request:
    branches: ["**"]
  schedule:
    # Run weekly on Monday at 02:00 UTC (simulates scheduled cleanup)
    - cron: "0 2 * * 1"
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      dry_run:
        description: "Run in dry-run mode (no actual deletions)"
        required: false
        default: "true"

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Job 1: Run Pester unit tests for the cleanup script
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  test:
    name: Run Pester Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install PowerShell
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update -q
          sudo apt-get install -y powershell

      - name: Install Pester
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted
          Install-Module -Name Pester -MinimumVersion 5.0.0 -Force -Scope CurrentUser

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $config = New-PesterConfiguration
          $config.Run.Path = './ArtifactCleanup.Tests.ps1'
          $config.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          $config.TestResult.Enabled = $true
          $config.TestResult.OutputPath = 'test-results.xml'
          $config.TestResult.OutputFormat = 'NUnitXml'
          $result = Invoke-Pester -Configuration $config
          if ($result.FailedCount -gt 0) {
            Write-Error "Pester tests failed: $($result.FailedCount) failure(s)"
            exit 1
          }
          Write-Host "All $($result.PassedCount) tests passed."

      - name: Upload test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: pester-test-results
          path: test-results.xml

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Job 2: Demonstrate the cleanup script with mock artifact data
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:

name: Artifact Cleanup Script

on:
  push:
    branches: ["**"]
  pull_request:
    branches: ["**"]
  schedule:
    # Run weekly on Monday at 02:00 UTC (simulates scheduled cleanup)
    - cron: "0 2 * * 1"
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      dry_run:
        description: "Run in dry-run mode (no actual deletions)"
        required: false
        default: "true"

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Job 1: Run Pester unit tests for the cleanup script
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  test:
    name: Run Pester Tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install PowerShell
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update -q
          sudo apt-get install -y powershell

      - name: Install Pester
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          Set-PSRepository -Name PSGallery -InstallationPolicy Trusted
          Install-Module -Name Pester -MinimumVersion 5.0.0 -Force -Scope CurrentUser

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $config = New-PesterConfiguration
          $config.Run.Path = './ArtifactCleanup.Tests.ps1'
          $config.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          $config.TestResult.Enabled = $true
          $config.TestResult.OutputPath = 'test-results.xml'
          $config.TestResult.OutputFormat = 'NUnitXml'
          $result = Invoke-Pester -Configuration $config
          if ($result.FailedCount -gt 0) {
            Write-Error "Pester tests failed: $($result.FailedCount) failure(s)"
            exit 1
          }
          Write-Host "All $($result.PassedCount) tests passed."

      - name: Upload test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: pester-test-results
          path: test-results.xml

  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Job 2: Demonstrate the cleanup script with mock artifact data
  # -----------------------------------------------------------------------
  demonstrate:
    name: Demonstrate Artifact Cleanup
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: test

    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install PowerShell
        run: |
          sudo apt-get update -q
          sudo apt-get install -y powershell

      - name: Run artifact cleanup (dry-run)
        shell: pwsh
        env:
          DRY_RUN: ${{ github.event.inputs.dry_run || 'true' }}
        run: |
          # Source the cleanup functions
          . ./ArtifactCleanup.ps1

          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          # Mock artifact data representing a typical CI artifact store
          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          $referenceDate = [datetime]"2026-04-09"

          $artifacts = @(
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-001"    -SizeBytes 52428800  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2025-12-01") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-002"    -SizeBytes 55574528  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-01-15") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-003"    -SizeBytes 57671680  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-02-20") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-004"    -SizeBytes 60817408  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-03-10") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "build-main-005"    -SizeBytes 62914560  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-04-01") -WorkflowRunId "ci-build"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "test-report-001"   -SizeBytes 10485760  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-01-20") -WorkflowRunId "ci-test"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "test-report-002"   -SizeBytes 11534336  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-03-15") -WorkflowRunId "ci-test"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "test-report-003"   -SizeBytes 12582912  -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-04-05") -WorkflowRunId "ci-test"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "deploy-staging-01" -SizeBytes 209715200 -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2025-11-01") -WorkflowRunId "cd-deploy"
            New-ArtifactRecord -Name "deploy-prod-01"    -SizeBytes 209715200 -CreatedAt ([datetime]"2026-04-08") -WorkflowRunId "cd-deploy"
          )

          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          # Retention policy
          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          $policy = @{
            MaxAgeDays        = 90        # delete anything older than 90 days
            MaxTotalSizeBytes = 500MB     # keep total under 500 MB
            KeepLatestN       = 3         # keep at most 3 artifacts per workflow
          }

          $dryRun = ($env:DRY_RUN -eq 'true')

          $plan   = Invoke-ArtifactCleanup -Artifacts $artifacts -Policy $policy -ReferenceDate $referenceDate -DryRun $dryRun
          $report = Format-CleanupReport -Plan $plan

          Write-Host $report

          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          # Emit structured output for downstream assertion
          # ------------------------------------------------------------------
          Write-Host "::notice title=Cleanup Summary::Deleted=$($plan.Summary.ArtifactsDeleted) Retained=$($plan.Summary.ArtifactsRetained) SpaceReclaimed=$($plan.Summary.SpaceReclaimedBytes)"
          Write-Host "ARTIFACTS_DELETED=$($plan.Summary.ArtifactsDeleted)"
          Write-Host "ARTIFACTS_RETAINED=$($plan.Summary.ArtifactsRetained)"
          Write-Host "SPACE_RECLAIMED_BYTES=$($plan.Summary.SpaceReclaimedBytes)"
